DMLearner: Double machine learning (EconML econml.dml.DML family)

Source: R/DMLearner.R
DMLearner.Rd

Special case of an R-learner style two-stage procedure: cross-fit nuisances \(\hat{q}(X) \approx E[Y \mid X]\) and \(\hat{f}(X) \approx E[T \mid X]\), form residuals \(\tilde{Y} = Y - \hat{q}(X)\), \(\tilde{T} = T - \hat{f}(X)\), then fit a CATE model solving (linear parametric case) \(\hat{\theta} = \arg\min E_n[(\tilde{Y} - \Theta(\phi(X)) \cdot \tilde{T})^2]\) i.e. OLS on the cross-product design \(\tilde{T} \otimes \phi(X)\) (EconML cross_product; Python _FinalWrapper._combine). Discrete treatment uses classification-style \(\hat{f}\) (probabilities / residualized binary \(T\)); continuous \(T\) uses regression for \(\hat{f}\).

Usage

DMLearner(
  model_y = "ranger",
  model_t = "ranger",
  model_final = "lm",
  fit_cate_intercept = TRUE,
  control_name = 0,
  n_fold = 5L,
  ate_alpha = 0.05,
  seed = NULL,
  alpha_glmnet = 1,
  kernel_dim = 20L,
  kernel_bw = 1
)

Arguments

model_y

character; first-stage outcome model ("lm", "ranger", "glmnet", "xgb") — EconML model_y

model_t

character; first-stage treatment model — EconML model_t (logistic-type when \(T\) is binary/discrete)

model_final

character; final CATE stage ("lm", "glmnet", "ranger", "xgb", "kernel", "causal_forest") — EconML model_final

fit_cate_intercept

logical; intercept in \(\phi(X)\) for linear final stages (EconML fit_cate_intercept)

control_name

baseline level for discrete \(T\) (EconML categories first level)

n_fold

cross-fitting folds (EconML cv)

ate_alpha

confidence level for ATE intervals

seed

random seed for fold assignment (random_state)

alpha_glmnet

L1 mix for SparseLinearDML / glmnet final

kernel_dim, kernel_bw

random-feature kernel (EconML KernelDML)

Value

Object of class DMLearner; fitted nuisances in models_y, models_t; final fit in model_cate (cf. EconML model_cate / model_final_).

Details

**Multi-treatment:** pass treatment as an \(n \times d\) matrix or data frame (\(d > 1\)). First stage fits \(E[T_j \mid X]\) per column; final stage is joint regression on dml_joint_res_design(res_t, phi). Only model_final "lm", "glmnet", "kernel".

**EconML not mirrored here (string API):** optional controls W in nuisances (use cbind(X,W) manually); featurizer / treatment_featurizer transformers; mc_iters / mc_agg; discrete_outcome; sample_weight / freq_weight / sample_var in fit; sensitivity_summary; Ray. Extensions can follow the same residual-on-residual structure as in econml/dml/dml.py.

References

Chernozhukov V, Chetverikov D, Demirer M, Duflo E, Hansen C, Newey W (2016). Double/debiased machine learning for treatment and causal parameters. arXiv:1608.00060.

Nie X, Wager S (2021). Quasi-oracle estimation of heterogeneous treatment effects. Biometrika 108(2), 299–319. (R-learner / orthogonal learner family.)

EconML: https://github.com/py-why/EconML — module econml.dml (dml.py).

DoubleML (R package): https://docs.doubleml.org

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# DMLearner(...)
} # }
